a:r.a:t: The skills subsystem has dependency graph validation via dependency_graph.py and quality telemetry via telemetry.py, but three governance gaps remain. First, cryptographic signing -- skill packs use SHA256 checksums for integrity, but not asymmetric signatures for authenticity. A compromised database could inject a malicious skill that passes the checksum. The fix is asymmetric key signing with chain-of-custody tracking: author, approver, and timestamp on every skill pack. Second, prompt versioning -- system prompts are the single most important control surface for agent behavior, yet they are stored as inline strings with no version history, no diff view, and no rollback capability. A prompt change that degrades quality is invisible until users complain. The planned prompts/registry.py would add versioned prompt storage with SHA-based IDs, diff comparison, canary rollout at 10 percent traffic, and automatic rollback on quality regression. Third, structured output guardrails -- agent outputs are free-form text with no schema validation or content filtering. Agents can return malformed JSON, leak PII, or produce off-brand content. The planned runtime/guardrails.py would enforce Pydantic output schemas per agent mode and add content policy filters for PII, toxicity, and brand compliance with automatic retry on validation failure.
